program: —  (Mirror held-out scale-up: obscure + stack-diverse, Graphify path)

reference_claims.text: Module kotlinx-schema-annotations exists, providing runtime annotations (@Schema, @Description).
Module kotlinx-schema-json exists, providing type-safe models and a DSL for building JSON Schema definitions.
Module kotlinx-schema-generator-core exists, providing the internal representation (IR), introspection utils, and generator interfaces.
Module kotlinx-schema-generator-json exists, providing the JSON Schema transformer from the IR plus a kotlinx-serialization schema generator.
Module kotlinx-schema-ksp exists, the KSP processor that scans code and generates extension properties.
A Gradle plugin module exists (the doc labels it 'kotlinx-schema-gradle-plugin'); it is the Gradle plugin with id org.jetbrains.kotlinx.schema.ksp.
Module gradle-plugin-integration-tests exists, an independent build including the main project demonstrating real MPP usage.
Module ksp-integration-tests exists, KSP end-to-end tests for generation without the Gradle plugin.
TypeGraph exists as the unified Internal Representation type.
TypeGraph is the unified intermediate format carrying Properties, Types, Descriptions, Nullable, and Defaults; a graph of discovered types plus a root reference.
SchemaIntrospector is the introspector abstraction that extracts type information and produces a TypeGraph (introspect(root): TypeGraph).
A KSP (compile-time) introspector exists that turns Kotlin declarations into the TypeGraph IR (doc box 'KspSchemaIntrospector').
A runtime reflection introspector exists that turns (e.g. Java/third-party) classes into the TypeGraph IR (doc box 'ReflectionSchemaIntrospector').
SerializationClassSchemaIntrospector is a runtime introspector that converts kotlinx.serialization SerialDescriptor instances into the TypeGraph IR.
TypeGraphTransformer is the transformer abstraction that converts a TypeGraph into a target schema format (transform(graph, rootName)).
A JSON Schema transformer (doc box 'JsonSchemaTransformer') converts the TypeGraph into JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12).
A Function Calling transformer (doc box 'FunctionCallingTransformer', OpenAI/Anthropic format) converts the TypeGraph into a function-calling schema.
SchemaGeneratorService discovers and looks up SchemaGenerators by target type T and schema class via getGenerator(T::class, R::class).
SchemaGenerator generates a schema (R) for a target T by invoking an introspector (TypeGraph) then a transformer.
The runtime workflow is: Service.getGenerator -> Generator.generate(T) -> Introspector.introspect(T)=TypeGraph -> Transformer.transform(TypeGraph, rootName)=schema(R).
Both KSP (compile-time) and reflection/serialization (runtime) sources are unified by translating into the shared TypeGraph IR, decoupling sources from output targets.
kotlinx-schema-generator-json depends on (uses) kotlinx-schema-generator-core.
kotlinx-schema-generator-json depends on (uses) kotlinx-schema-json.
kotlinx-schema-generator-core depends on (knows) kotlinx-schema-annotations.
kotlinx-schema-ksp depends on (uses) kotlinx-schema-generator-json.
The Gradle plugin uses kotlinx-schema-ksp (wires it in as the KSP processor dependency for consumer builds).
The KSP path has zero runtime overhead because @Schema/@Description are compile-time (SOURCE-retention) annotations consumed at compile time.
The library is Kotlin Multiplatform, supporting JVM, JS, Native, and Wasm targets.
The JSON Schema output conforms to JSON Schema Draft 2020-12.
Schema output models (JsonSchema, FunctionCallingSchema) are kotlinx.serialization @Serializable types, and the JSON output ultimately surfaces as JsonObject / JSON string.
The KSP processor (SchemaExtensionProcessor) is the entry point that scans annotated code and generates KClass<T>.jsonSchema and KClass<T>.jsonSchemaString extension properties.
The Gradle plugin (id org.jetbrains.kotlinx.schema.ksp) auto-applies KSP, adds the processor dependency, and wires generated sources into the consumer's source sets.

emitted_claims.claim: kotlinx-schema-annotations is a Kotlin-multiplatform module defining the user-facing marker annotations that drive schema generation: @Schema, @Description, @SchemaIgnore.

@Schema is the entry-point annotation: applied to a CLASS or FUNCTION (Retention SOURCE) it marks the element for schema generation; value defaults to "json" and withSchemaObject defaults to false (whether to also emit a JsonObject/JsonSchema representation).

kotlinx-schema-generator-core is the format-agnostic core module: it defines the SchemaGenerator abstraction, the intermediate representation (TypeGraph IR), the introspector/transformer interfaces, and the JVM reflection introspectors.

SchemaGenerator<T,R> is the central public abstraction: declares targetType()/schemaType() (the registry keys), generateSchema(target):R, generateSchemaString(target):String, and encodeToString(schema):String.

The schema-generation pipeline is a fixed two-stage flow encoded in AbstractSchemaGenerator: generateSchema() ALWAYS calls introspector.introspect(target) to build a TypeGraph, then typeGraphTransformer.transform(graph, getRootName(target)) to produce the output. Every concrete generator is the composition of one SchemaIntrospector plus one TypeGraphTransformer.

SchemaIntrospector<T,C> is the front-half facade: it reads a source (KClass via reflection, KSP declaration, or kotlinx.serialization SerialDescriptor) and produces a language-agnostic TypeGraph via introspect(root). Each implementation carries its own config:C.

TypeGraphTransformer<R,C> is the back-half: it converts the TypeGraph IR into a concrete target schema format (JSON Schema, function-calling schema, etc.) via transform(graph, rootName), guided by its config. AbstractTypeGraphTransformer is the base class.

TypeGraph is the immutable intermediate representation that decouples introspection from emission: a data class holding root:TypeRef plus nodes:Map<TypeId,TypeNode>. TypeId is the stable identifier used for deduplication and $ref linking; TypeNode is a sealed hierarchy (Primitive/Enum/Object/List/Map/Any/Polymorphic) and TypeRef is sealed Inline|Ref. This IR is the contract that lets any introspector pair with any transformer.

ReflectionClassIntrospector (JVM-only, a singleton object) introspects a KClass at runtime via kotlin-reflect, walking properties/constructors into a TypeGraph; it requires a primary constructor and does not fully support type parameters.

SchemaGeneratorService is the JVM runtime registry/entry point: a singleton object that uses java.util.ServiceLoader to discover all SchemaGenerator implementations at load time, and getGenerator(targetType, schemaType) selects one by its (targetType, schemaType) pair.

Generator lookup must be unambiguous: SchemaGeneratorService.getGenerator filters by targetType then uses singleOrNull on schemaType, so a (targetType, schemaType) pair must resolve to exactly one registered generator or the lookup returns null (singleOrNull throws on >1 match).

@InternalSchemaGeneratorApi is a @RequiresOptIn marker fencing off the internal schema-generation surface; downstream modules must opt in. The core, json-generator, and ksp build files all declare optIn for kotlinx.schema.generator.core.InternalSchemaGeneratorApi, making it the enforced internal/public boundary.

kotlinx-schema-json is a Kotlin-multiplatform module defining the serializable JSON Schema data model (JsonSchema, FunctionCallingSchema, AdditionalPropertiesConstraint) and its kotlinx.serialization custom serializers.

kotlinx-schema-json owns the output schema vocabulary: the JsonSchema model is @Serializable with bespoke serializers (e.g. AdditionalPropertiesSerializer, PolymorphicEnumSerializer, StringOrListSerializer) so it round-trips to/from spec-shaped JSON Schema; AdditionalPropertiesConstraint encodes the true/false/schema tri-state.

kotlinx-schema-generator-json is the JSON-Schema-specific generator module: it provides the JsonSchemaGenerator interface, the TypeGraph->JSON transformers, and the reflection- and serialization-based concrete generators.

JsonSchemaGenerator<T> specializes SchemaGenerator<T, JsonObject> and fixes encodeToString to kotlinx.serialization Json.encodeToString(schema) — binding the JSON-Schema output format to the kotlinx.serialization Json encoder.

ReflectionClassJsonSchemaGenerator is the runtime-reflection JSON-Schema generator: it composes ReflectionClassIntrospector (KClass->TypeGraph) with TypeGraphToJsonSchemaTransformer (TypeGraph->JsonSchema), targetType()=KClass, schemaType()=JsonSchema, and ships a Default instance using JsonSchemaConfig.Strict and Json{encodeDefaults=false}.

SerializationClassJsonSchemaGenerator is the second runtime path: it composes SerializationClassSchemaIntrospector (kotlinx.serialization SerialDescriptor -> TypeGraph) with TypeGraphToJsonSchemaTransformer, so a JSON Schema can be derived from a kotlinx.serialization descriptor instead of reflection; targetType()=SerialDescriptor.
kotlinx-schema-ksp is the compile-time path: a JVM module hosting the KSP SymbolProcessor that generates schema extension properties at build time for elements annotated @Schema.

SchemaExtensionProcessorProvider is the KSP entry point registered in META-INF/services (SymbolProcessorProvider); its create() instantiates SchemaExtensionProcessor with the KSP codeGenerator, logger and options.

SchemaExtensionProcessor is the compile-time processor: per round it reads symbols annotated kotlinx.schema.Schema, filters them via SymbolFilter, and for each valid class/function generates an extension property (e.g. `val MyClass.jsonSchemaString: String`). Classes use ClassSchemaStrategy; functions dispatch across TopLevel/Instance/Companion/Object function strategies.
@Schema and @SchemaIgnore are mutually exclusive on the same declaration: the processor logs an error and skips the class (no schema emitted) when both are present — an enforced consistency rule.

Schema generation can be globally disabled and scoped via KSP options: OPTION_ENABLED (kotlinx.schema.enabled=="false" disables all generation), OPTION_ROOT_PACKAGE, OPTION_INCLUDE / OPTION_EXCLUDE (comma/semicolon glob lists, exclude wins over include), OPTION_VISIBILITY, and OPTION_WITH_SCHEMA_OBJECT.

Per-symbol failure is isolated: SchemaExtensionProcessor wraps each class/function generation in try/catch and logs an error rather than aborting the round, and declarations that fail validate() are returned as deferred (unprocessable) for the next KSP round.

UnifiedKspSchemaGenerator collapses class- and function-schema generation into one config-driven AbstractSchemaGenerator: the KspSchemaGeneratorConfig supplies the introspector (KspClassIntrospector vs KspFunctionIntrospector), the transformer (TypeGraphToJsonObjectSchemaTransformer vs TypeGraphToFunctionCallingSchemaTransformer), and JSON formatting. targetType()/schemaType() intentionally throw because KSP uses compile-time symbols, not runtime KClass.

KspIntrospectionContext is the shared KSP introspection engine: it extends BaseIntrospectionContext (inheriting node-state management and cycle detection) and applies an ordered type-resolution strategy (basic/primitive+collection -> Any fallback for generics -> sealed->Polymorphic -> enum->Enum -> object->ObjectNode), eliminating toRef() duplication between the class and function introspectors.

kotlinx-schema-ksp-gradle-plugin is a java-gradle-plugin module that packages the KSP processor as a consumable Gradle plugin (id org.jetbrains.kotlinx.schema.ksp, implementation class kotlinx.schema.ksp.gradle.plugin.KotlinxSchemaPlugin).

KotlinxSchemaPlugin is the Gradle build-time entry point: apply(Project) registers the kotlinxSchema DSL extension, short-circuits if enabled==false, then detects the Kotlin plugin type and routes to MultiplatformConfigurator or JvmConfigurator. It invokes KSP2 programmatically (KotlinSymbolProcessing) instead of applying Google's KSP Gradle plugin.

KspTask is annotated @CacheableTask and documented as configuration-cache compatible: all inputs are resolved in the configuration phase, the task action holds no Project/Configuration references, and it relies on KSP being deterministic (same sources+classpath+options => same generated output under build/generated/kotlinxSchema/).

Module layering is declared via Gradle project() dependencies: kotlinx-schema-generator-json api-depends on kotlinx-schema-annotations, kotlinx-schema-generator-core, and kotlinx-schema-json (the json generator sits atop core + the json model + annotations).

kotlinx-schema-generator-core api-depends on kotlinx-schema-annotations (and on kotlinx.serialization.json), so the format-agnostic core layers directly on the annotation contract; it does NOT depend on the json-generator or ksp modules (one-directional layering).

kotlinx-schema-ksp implementation-depends on kotlinx-schema-generator-json (plus the KSP api), so the compile-time processor reuses the JSON generator stack; the dependency points ksp -> generator-json -> generator-core (never the reverse).

The repo is a Gradle multi-module Kotlin Multiplatform build: settings.gradle.kts includes the 7 library/test modules and docs, enables TYPESAFE_PROJECT_ACCESSORS, and resolves dependencies from mavenCentral; shared build logic lives in the buildSrc convention plugins (kotlin-multiplatform-convention, kotlin-jvm-convention, dokka-convention, publishing-convention).

kotlinx.serialization is the serialization backbone: generator-core, generator-json and the json model all api-depend on kotlinx.serialization.json, the JSON model is @Serializable with custom KSerializers, and generators apply the kotlin("plugin.serialization") Gradle plugin.

Two distinct introspection technologies back the two run paths: KSP (Kotlin Symbol Processing, com.google.devtools.ksp) for compile-time generation in the ksp module, and kotlin-reflect for runtime generation in generator-core jvmMain (jvmMain dependency implementation(kotlin("reflect"))).

Logging uses io.github.oshai kotlin-logging (KotlinLogging) — e.g. SchemaGeneratorService logs discovered generators at init; the dependency is declared as implementation(libs.kotlin.logging) in generator-core.

ksp-integration-tests and gradle-plugin-integration-tests are dedicated end-to-end test fixtures: the former exercises the KSP processor over model/function fixtures, the latter applies the actual Gradle plugin to jvm-module and kmp-module sub-projects to verify schema generation across JVM and multiplatform.

KspTaskConfiguration/KspTask wire the generated sources to a fixed output location: the plugin places generated files under build/generated/kotlinxSchema/, the contract by which consuming compilations pick up the generated extension properties.

adjudication.note: EC-01 names the annotations module + @Schema/@Description (and @SchemaIgnore).
EC-13 = kotlinx-schema-json serializable JSON Schema data model.
EC-03 = generator-core: SchemaGenerator, TypeGraph IR, introspector/transformer interfaces.
EC-15 = generator-json: JsonSchemaGenerator, TypeGraph->JSON transformers, serialization-based generators.
EC-19 = kotlinx-schema-ksp KSP SymbolProcessor module.
EC-27 = gradle-plugin module, plugin id org.jetbrains.kotlinx.schema.ksp, KotlinxSchemaPlugin; matches the role/id (doc's module label divergence is cosmetic, same as RC currency note).
EC-37 = gradle-plugin-integration-tests applies the actual plugin to jvm/kmp sub-projects (independent build, MPP usage).
EC-37 also names ksp-integration-tests as KSP processor e2e fixtures (without the Gradle plugin).
EC-08 declares TypeGraph as the IR data class (root + nodes); entails the component-exists fact (kind label differs, fact-keyed credit).

EC-08 captures TypeGraph as graph-of-discovered-types (nodes:Map<TypeId,TypeNode>) + root:TypeRef; the carried metadata (nullable/defaults/descriptions/properties) is the node hierarchy. Fact-keyed data-store match.
EC-06 = SchemaIntrospector facade, introspect(root)->TypeGraph.
EC-26 names KspClassIntrospector/KspFunctionIntrospector + KspIntrospectionContext as the compile-time KSP introspection; bridges the doc's conceptual KspSchemaIntrospector box.
EC-09 = ReflectionClassIntrospector, JVM runtime kotlin-reflect -> TypeGraph; bridges doc's ReflectionSchemaIntrospector box.
EC-18 names SerializationClassSchemaIntrospector (SerialDescriptor->TypeGraph) as the runtime serialization introspector — exact-name match.
EC-07 = TypeGraphTransformer, transform(graph,rootName)->R, AbstractTypeGraphTransformer base.
EC-17 (and EC-25) name TypeGraphToJsonSchemaTransformer (TypeGraph->JsonSchema) — the JSON Schema transformer role; bridges doc's JsonSchemaTransformer box.
EC-25 names TypeGraphToFunctionCallingSchemaTransformer as the function-calling transformer option; bridges doc's FunctionCallingTransformer box.
EC-10 = SchemaGeneratorService ServiceLoader registry, getGenerator(targetType,schemaType) lookup. Fact-keyed (kind label entry-point vs component-role).
EC-04 (SchemaGenerator<T,R> abstraction) + EC-05 (generateSchema = introspect then transform) capture the generator producing R for T via introspector+transformer.
EC-05 = the fixed pipeline getGenerator -> introspect(target)=TypeGraph -> transform(graph,rootName)=output; combined with EC-10/EC-04 captures the full workflow invariant.

EC-08 ('IR is the contract that lets any introspector pair with any transformer') + EC-05 ('every generator = one introspector + one transformer') capture the source/target decoupling via shared TypeGraph.
EC-30 = generator-json api-depends on generator-core.
EC-30 = generator-json api-depends on kotlinx-schema-json.
EC-31 = generator-core api-depends on annotations.
EC-32 = ksp implementation-depends on generator-json.

No EC asserts the Gradle plugin -> kotlinx-schema-ksp processor-coordinate dependency. EC-28 says the plugin invokes KSP2 (the KSP API) programmatically, not that it adds the kotlinx-schema-ksp processor dep; EC-30/31/32 cover other edges. Referent (KspTaskBuilder.kt) is readable -> miss_referent_read=true.

The constituent code facts of the zero-runtime-overhead boundary are emitted: EC-02 (@Schema Retention SOURCE) + EC-19 (ksp is the compile-time/build-time path). Fact-keyed credit for the SOURCE-retention/compile-time-consumed boundary.

EC-33 = Gradle multi-module Kotlin Multiplatform build (kotlin-multiplatform-convention); EC-01/EC-13 also call modules KMP. The KMP technology choice is captured; the JVM/JS/Native/Wasm target enumeration is a sub-detail not separately spelled out.

No EC names JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. EC-14/16/17 mention JSON Schema output generally but never the draft version. Referent (TypeGraphToJsonSchemaTransformer KDoc/JSON_SCHEMA_ID_DRAFT202012) is readable -> miss_referent_read=true.
EC-13/EC-14 (JsonSchema + FunctionCallingSchema are @Serializable, custom serializers) + EC-16 (output is JsonObject) capture the serializable-models-surface-as-JsonObject/JSON fact.

EC-21 = processor generates extension property e.g. val MyClass.jsonSchemaString: String (via ClassSchemaStrategy); EC-19/EC-20 the KSP entry point. jsonSchema JsonObject variant implied via withSchemaObject (EC-02/EC-23).
EC-28 = KotlinxSchemaPlugin.apply() build-time entry point (DSL, configurator routing, KSP2 invocation) + EC-38 wires generated sources into build/generated/kotlinxSchema/.

beyond_doc.note: code-groundable ECs absent from the doc (NEUTRAL)

scoring_provenance.fact_keyed: MATCH ec_kind normalized to rc_kind (matcher authoritative)

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
